articles.title: Ontario’s Minister of Sport Neil Lumsden announces retirement
articles.summary: Ontario’s Minister of Sport Neil Lumsden announced Friday that he is retiring from Premier Doug Ford’s cabinet and the provincial legislature, effective Aug. 4. Lumsden, a former football player and manager in the Canadian Football League, has been the MPP for Hamilton East-Stoney Creek since 2022. “To the people of Hamilton East-Stoney Creek, thank you […]

articles.title: Crash between scooter, vehicle near Dufferin Mall leaves man dead: Toronto police
articles.summary: A man has died in hospital after a collision involving a motorized scooter and a sedan near Bloor Street W. and Dufferin Street Friday morning, Toronto police say.

articles.title: 3 more suspects wanted after teen stabbed at Taste of Lawrence festival
articles.summary: Toronto police say they are searching for three additional suspects after a teen was stabbed at the Taste of Lawrence festival earlier this month. Two 16-year-old boys from Toronto have already been arrested in the alleged incident that occurred on Saturday, July 4. Officers were called to the area of Lawrence Avenue East and Birchmount […]

articles.title: Child among 4 to hospital after multi‑vehicle crash closes Hwy. 2A in Scarborough
articles.summary: A multi‑vehicle collision in Scarborough sent four people to hospital Friday morning and forced the closure of a major stretch of Hwy. 2A, Toronto police and paramedics say. Emergency crews were called to Hwy. 2A and Meadowvale Road around 9:39 a.m. for reports of a crash involving multiple vehicles. Police arrived to find significant damage […]

articles.title: Kitchener man charged after counterfeit cash allegedly used to buy gold bars in online marketplace scams
articles.summary: Peel Regional Police (PRP) have charged a 24‑year‑old Kitchener man in connection with two alleged scams involving counterfeit currency used to purchase precious metals from private sellers on online marketplaces. The Fraud Bureau launched the investigation after two sellers reported being defrauded during arranged meetups for gold and silver sales. Police say the first incident […]

articles.title: Male scooter operator dies following crash near Bloor and Dufferin
articles.summary: A man who was operating a scooter has been pronounced dead following a crash involving a vehicle in Toronto’s west end. Emergency crews were called to the area of Bloor Street West and Dufferin Street near Dufferin Mall just after 8 a.m. on Friday. Paramedics say they transported a man to hospital with life-threatening injuries, […]

articles.title: Windows smashed, graffiti sprayed at Vaughan business as police search for suspects
articles.summary: York Regional Police (YRP) are searching for two suspects after a business in Vaughan was vandalized last weekend, with windows smashed and graffiti sprayed across the property. Investigators say officers were called to the area of Langstaff Road and Dufferin Street near Hwy. 7 around 5 a.m. on July 12 after reports of damage to […]

articles.title: Mosquitos in York, Halton regions test positive for West Nile virus
articles.summary: York Region Public Health and Halton Region have confirmed some mosquitos in the regions have tested positive for West Nile virus.

articles.title: Body of Brampton man found in Stoney Creek culvert, police say
articles.summary: Hamilton police have identified a body found this week in a culvert in Stoney Creek, Ont., as a Brampton man who had also lived in Halifax. Taranpreet Singh Sidhu, 29, had immigrated to Canada from India in 2022. Anyone who had contact with him before his death are asked to contact police.

articles.title: Markham is hosting an IndyCar race next month. Is the city ready?
articles.summary: The Ontario Honda Dealers Indy at Markham will take place in the city from Friday, Aug. 14 to Sunday, Aug. 16, and the city says it’s ready to welcome the drivers, along with tens of thousands of race fans.
